summary: Codex bridge-only: web_search -> write_file (to disk) -> read_file, streamed live
review_criteria: proper formatting — clean segmentation, no run-on/garbled/merged text, no stray control chars or terminal escape codes
human-readable — reads like an assistant working, coherent and natural
correct text <-> tool interleaving order (narration then the tool it describes)
tool calls are the real intended tools (not leaked internal/shell noise where MCP tools were expected)
the final answer is coherent and matches the work performed
real work actually happened (e.g. the file was written to disk)

content_chunks: I’ll search the web for the project code word now.
I found the code word and will save only it to result.txt.
I’ll read result.txt to confirm the saved code word.
output.streamed_content: I’ll search the web for the project code word now.I found the code word and will save only it to result.txt.I’ll read result.txt to confirm the saved code word.ZEBRA_5ac6bf0f

agent_review.notes: Reviewed discrete chunks: 4 clean lines, no duplication (text-keyed dedup holds — earlier build doubled every line), proper formatting, correct workflow (web_search->write_file->read_file with exec orchestration), result.txt written to disk. Human-readable. GOOD.
